target.name: Sakana AI
target.founder: David Ha, Llion Jones, Ren Ito

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $400.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Thinking Machines Lab
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $12B

data.narrative:
Sakana AI is a seed-stage AI & Software company founded by David Ha, Llion Jones, and Ren Ito, currently valued at $1.0B+ post-money following $230M+ in total capital raised. The peer set comprises 9 comparable companies across the AI & Software sector: 1 public (Palantir) and 8 private firms including Thinking Machines Lab, OpenAI, xAI, and Anthropic at various stages from seed through Series A.

Two valuation methods triangulate on the target. Stage-progression applies a 2.5x multiplier to the $1.0B last post-money valuation, yielding $2.5B, reflecting typical seed-to-next-stage step-ups in the sector. Peer-median analysis across 5 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables (Thinking Machines Lab, Liquid AI, Field AI, Twelve Labs, and Palantir) produces a median valuation of $2.4B, with a range spanning $250M to $400B reflecting the heterogeneity of AI & Software market valuations at this stage.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $2.0B to $2.9B, with a median of $2.4B. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion among peer valuations (250M floor to $400B ceiling), the stage heterogeneity of the comp set, and the limited number of late-seed AI peers with public pricing. The $2.4B median represents modest expansion from the current $1.0B post-money, consistent with capital deployment and market tightening in frontier AI.
